# NutriThrive

> Lab-tested moringa powder, Darjeeling black tea, dried curry leaves and natural soaps. Packed and shipped from Truganina, Melbourne VIC. Food supplements — not medical advice.

## Products

- [Moringa Leaf Powder](https://nutrithrive.com.au/products/moringa-powder/): NMI Government lab-tested, shade-dried, $11/100g or $19/200g. No fillers, no additives.
- [Darjeeling Black Tea](https://nutrithrive.com.au/products/black-tea/): First-flush Darjeeling, rich muscatel aroma, $7.50/50g.
- [Dried Curry Leaves](https://nutrithrive.com.au/products/curry-leaves/): Whole-leaf, shade-dried, $5.50/20g.
- [Moringa Soap](https://nutrithrive.com.au/products/moringa-soap/): Handmade natural bar, $7.
- [Wellness Combo Pack](https://nutrithrive.com.au/products/combo-pack/): Moringa powder + tea + curry leaves bundle.
- [All products](https://nutrithrive.com.au/products/): Full catalogue with pricing.

## Key pages

- [Home](https://nutrithrive.com.au/): Overview of products, lab-test credentials and free-shipping threshold.
- [About](https://nutrithrive.com.au/about): Founder Neer, Truganina warehouse, sourcing story.
- [FAQ](https://nutrithrive.com.au/faq): Dosage, storage, shipping, TGA and legal questions answered.
- [Blog](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/): Buying guides, brand comparisons and moringa research.
- [Contact](https://nutrithrive.com.au/contact): Email and street address.
- [Shipping & Returns](https://nutrithrive.com.au/pages/shipping/shipping-returns): Rates, free-shipping threshold ($80 AUD), return policy.

## Buying guides & comparisons

- [How to choose moringa powder in Australia](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/how-to-choose-moringa-powder-australia-2026): Colour, shade-drying, lab tests, where to buy, capsules vs powder, and 2026 brand price snapshot.
- [How long does moringa powder last?](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/how-long-does-moringa-powder-last-storage-shelf-life-2026): Shelf life, pantry storage, why not to refrigerate, and signs it's time to replace a bag.
- [Moringa soap benefits for skin](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-soap-benefits-for-skin-2026): Honest look at what moringa soap can offer skin — and what's marketing overreach (2026).
- [Best time to take moringa powder](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/best-time-to-take-moringa-powder-morning-or-night-2026): Morning vs night, with food or without — what timing actually matters and what's wellness-blog filler.
- [How to add moringa to your diet](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/how-to-add-moringa-to-diet): Practical everyday ways to use moringa powder.
- [Chemist Warehouse moringa vs NutriThrive](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-chemist-warehouse-vs-nutrithrive-quality-test-2025): Honest side-by-side quality test.
- [Rosabella moringa review](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/rosabella-moringa-reviews-legit-or-overhyped-2026): Independent review of a competing brand.
- [AG1 vs moringa powder](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/ag1-alternative-australia-moringa-comparison-2026): Is a $150 greens powder worth it, or is moringa enough?
- [Moringa vs Spirulina vs Matcha](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-vs-spirulina-vs-matcha-comparison-australia): Plant-based superfoods compared for Australian buyers (includes curry leaves note).
- [Shade-dried vs sun-dried moringa](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/science-shade-drying-vs-sun-drying-moringa): Quality and nutrient-retention science compared.
- [Darjeeling black tea Australia — first flush vs second flush](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/darjeeling-black-tea-australia-first-flush-second-flush-guide-2026): Flush types, caffeine, brewing, and how to spot real Darjeeling.
- [How much caffeine in Darjeeling tea?](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/how-much-caffeine-in-darjeeling-tea-vs-coffee-green-tea-2026): Caffeine per cup compared to coffee and green tea, what affects it, and whether it's a realistic morning swap (2026).

## Health topics

- [Moringa benefits for women](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-benefits-for-women-comprehensive-2026): Iron, hormones and skin — evidence summary for Australian women.
- [Moringa for breastfeeding](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-for-breastfeeding-milk-supply-2026): Milk supply research, leaf-only safety, dosage, and what to ask your midwife (2026).
- [Iron deficiency in Australian women](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/iron-deficiency-australian-women-symptoms-plant-based-sources-2026): Symptoms, causes and plant-based food sources (2026).
- [Moringa side effects](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-side-effects-what-happens-take-too-much-2026): Safe dosage, what too much moringa does, and who should avoid it.
- [Is moringa safe for dogs?](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/is-moringa-safe-for-dogs-benefits-dosage-australia-2026): Benefits, dosage by weight, side effects, and the diabetes caveat for Australian pet owners.
- [Moringa and blood sugar](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-and-blood-sugar-diabetes-research-2026): What the research actually shows — mixed evidence, diabetes safety, and the honest takeaway (2026).
- [Moringa heavy metals lab testing Australia](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-heavy-metals-lab-testing-australia-what-to-look-for-2026): How to read CoA results — lead, cadmium, microbes (2026).
- [Moringa pregnancy safety Australia](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-pregnancy-safe-australia-trimester-guide-2026): Trimester guide — leaf vs root, GP-aligned cautions (2026).
- [Moringa smoothie recipes Australia](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-smoothie-recipes-australia-easy-2026): Teaspoon doses, three easy blends, clump-free tips (2026).
- [Is moringa banned in Australia?](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/is-moringa-banned-in-australia): 2026 Rosabella recall vs FSANZ food status — clarified.
- [Moringa and berberine together](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-and-berberine-australia-what-science-says-2026): What the science says about combining them (Australia 2026).
- [Cortisol cocktail trend explained](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/cortisol-cocktail-trend-explained-moringa-2026): Evidence-based look at where moringa fits in stress management.
- [GLP-1 / Ozempic nutrition gaps](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/glp-1-ozempic-nutrition-gaps-what-to-eat-2026): What to eat on GLP-1 medications to close nutrient gaps.
- [Dried curry leaves — tradition & health](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/nutrithrive-dried-curry-leaves-tradition-health): Traditional culinary uses and nutritional profile of curry leaves.
- [Can you use dried curry leaves for hair?](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/can-you-use-dried-curry-leaves-for-hair-2026): What changes with dried vs fresh leaves, evidence, and three hair methods (oil, rinse, mask).
- [What do curry leaves do for your health?](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/curry-leaves-health-benefits-what-the-evidence-says-2026): What's real, what's overstated, and eating vs cooking with curry leaves.
- [Moringa patches Australia review](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-patches-australia-review-do-they-work): Do patch supplements actually deliver moringa benefits?

## Untitled batch (June 2026)
- [Moringa for PCOS: What the Research Actually Shows](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-for-pcos-polycystic-ovary-syndrome-2026): A 2026 study explored moringa leaf extracts and PCOS. What the animal research found, what's still unproven in humans, and what PCOS symptoms moringa might support.
- [Moringa for High Blood Pressure: What the Evidence Shows](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-for-high-blood-pressure-hypertension-2026): Some research suggests moringa may mildly reduce blood pressure. An honest look at what's shown, what isn't, and the specific interaction that matters if you're on medication.
- [Moringa for Sleep: Does It Actually Help?](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-for-sleep-quality-insomnia-2026): Moringa and sleep quality — what the research shows, the specific nutrient mechanisms that might help, and why it's unlikely to work like melatonin.
- [Moringa for Period Pain and PMS: What Helps and What Doesn't](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-for-period-pain-pms-menstrual-health-2026): Can moringa reduce period pain or PMS symptoms? The iron and magnesium angle is real. The &quot;hormone balancing&quot; claims are overstated. Here's the honest breakdown.
- [Moringa Powder vs Moringa Capsules: Which Form Is Better?](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-powder-vs-capsules-comparison-2026): Should you buy moringa powder or capsules? They're the same ingredient in different formats. What's actually different, which is better value, and when each makes sense.
- [What Does Moringa Powder Taste Like? (Honest Guide + How to Hide It)](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/what-does-moringa-powder-taste-like-honest-guide-2026): Moringa powder tastes earthy, slightly bitter, sometimes peppery. An honest description plus exactly which foods and recipes mask it best — and which make it worse.
- [Moringa for Cholesterol: What the Research Says](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-for-cholesterol-heart-health-research-2026): Moringa and cholesterol — what animal and human research shows, what's still preliminary, and how it fits into a broader heart-healthy diet.
- [Why You Should Take Moringa with Vitamin C (and How To)](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-with-vitamin-c-iron-absorption-guide-2026): Moringa is iron-rich but plant-based iron absorbs poorly without vitamin C. The simple pairing that doubles how much iron you actually get from each teaspoon.
- [Moringa for Energy: What Actually Happens Week by Week](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-energy-what-happens-week-by-week-2026): Moringa doesn't give you immediate energy like caffeine. Here's the honest week-by-week picture of what actually changes, and why it takes longer than you expect.
- [Moringa Before or After a Workout: What Actually Makes Sense](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-before-after-workout-timing-guide-2026): When's the best time to take moringa around exercise? The iron and antioxidant angles are real. The pre-workout energy claim isn't. Here's what actually makes sense.
- [Moringa for Anxiety: What the Evidence Shows (Honest 2026 Guide)](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-for-anxiety-stress-evidence-2026): Moringa isn't a proven anxiety treatment. But some of its nutritional properties are genuinely relevant to stress and anxiety. Here's the honest breakdown.
- [Curry Leaves vs Curry Powder: They Are Not the Same Thing](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/curry-leaves-vs-curry-powder-difference-explained-2026): Curry leaves and curry powder are completely different ingredients that happen to share a name. What each actually is, why you can't swap one for the other, and what to use instead.
- [Curry Leaves Substitute: What to Use When You Don't Have Them](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/curry-leaves-substitute-what-to-use-2026): Don't have curry leaves for a recipe? The honest guide to substitutes that work, substitutes that sort of work, and the ones to skip entirely.
- [Fresh vs Dried Curry Leaves: Which Is Better for Cooking?](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/fresh-vs-dried-curry-leaves-cooking-comparison-2026): Fresh curry leaves taste stronger. Dried are more convenient. Which actually matters depends on what you're cooking. A practical comparison for Australian home cooks.
- [Curry Leaves for Heart Health and Cholesterol: What the Research Shows](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/curry-leaves-heart-health-cholesterol-evidence-2026): Curry leaves have been studied for effects on LDL cholesterol, triglycerides, and blood pressure. What the research actually shows and how it applies to everyday cooking.

- [Darjeeling Tea vs English Breakfast: What Is Actually Different?](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/darjeeling-tea-vs-english-breakfast-comparison-2026): Both are black teas. But Darjeeling and English Breakfast are completely different drinking experiences. A clear comparison on flavour, caffeine, strength, and when to use each. _(launches 14 July 2026)_
- [Is Darjeeling Tea a Good Coffee Replacement? An Honest Assessment](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/darjeeling-tea-coffee-replacement-honest-assessment-2026): People switch from coffee to Darjeeling for a lot of reasons. Whether it actually works as a replacement depends entirely on why you're making the switch. _(launches 14 July 2026)_
- [Can You Drink Darjeeling Tea Every Day? Benefits and Limits](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/can-you-drink-darjeeling-tea-every-day-2026): Yes, daily Darjeeling tea is fine for most people. What to know about caffeine limits, tannins and iron absorption, and the specific groups who should be more careful. _(launches 14 July 2026)_
- [Moringa Soap vs Regular Soap: What's Actually Different?](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/moringa-soap-vs-regular-soap-comparison-2026): Moringa soap versus standard commercial soap — what the actual differences are, what claims are real, and whether the premium is worth it for most people. _(launches 14 July 2026)_
- [Is Moringa Worth It? An Honest Cost-Per-Nutrient Breakdown for Australians](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/is-moringa-worth-it-cost-value-australia-2026): $11 for 100g — is moringa actually good value compared to a multivitamin, greens powder, or iron tablet? A real cost comparison of what you're getting per dollar. _(launches 14 July 2026)_

## Lifestyle health guides (July 2026)
- [Ultra-Processed Food in Australia: What It Actually Is and How Much You're Eating](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/ultra-processed-food-australia-what-it-means-how-much-you-eat-2026): Most Australians eat more ultra-processed food than they realise — and most don't know what the term actually means. A clear, non-preachy guide to what counts and what the research shows.
- [Signs You Might Be Magnesium Deficient (Most Australians Are)](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/signs-magnesium-deficiency-australia-what-to-eat-2026): Up to 80% of Australians may be magnesium deficient. The symptoms are easy to miss because they look like stress, poor sleep, and being busy. Here's what to look for.
- [Vitamin D Deficiency in Australia: The Paradox of the Sunniest Country](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/vitamin-d-deficiency-australia-sunny-country-paradox-2026): One in four Australians is vitamin D deficient in winter despite living in one of the sunniest countries on earth. Why this happens and what to do about it.
- [How to Build a Morning Routine That Actually Improves Your Health](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/morning-routine-health-tips-australia-2026): A morning routine doesn't have to take an hour. What the research supports, what's overrated, and a realistic version for people who are not morning people.
- [The Best Anti-Inflammatory Foods to Eat Every Day (Australian Guide 2026)](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/best-anti-inflammatory-foods-australia-daily-guide-2026): Chronic inflammation drives most of the major diseases Australians face. These are the foods with real, documented anti-inflammatory effects — and the ones that are hype.
- [Blood Sugar Spikes: What They Are and How to Avoid Them Through Food](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/blood-sugar-spikes-how-to-avoid-through-food-2026): Blood sugar spikes don't only affect diabetics. They affect everyone's energy, mood, hunger, and long-term health. What causes them and the simple food habits that help.
- [Why You're Always Tired: 7 Nutritional Deficiencies That Cause Fatigue in Australians](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/why-always-tired-nutritional-deficiencies-australia-2026): Persistent tiredness that sleep doesn't fix is often nutritional. Seven common deficiencies that cause fatigue in Australians — and how to identify which one applies to you.
- [How to Eat More Vegetables Without Changing Your Whole Diet](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/how-to-eat-more-vegetables-practical-guide-australia-2026): Most Australians don't eat enough vegetables. The good news is you don't need a meal plan or a new lifestyle. Practical ways to add vegetables to meals you already cook.
- [The Best Plant-Based Iron Foods for Australians (And How to Absorb Them Better)](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/best-plant-based-iron-foods-australia-absorption-guide-2026): Iron deficiency is one of the most common nutritional problems in Australia. For people eating less meat, knowing which plant foods actually deliver — and what to eat with them — matters.
- [How Much Water Should You Actually Drink Per Day? (The Honest Answer for Australians)](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/how-much-water-per-day-australians-honest-guide-2026): The &quot;eight glasses a day&quot; rule has almost no scientific basis. Here's what the actual evidence says about daily water intake, what counts toward it, and the signs you need more.

## Lifestyle health — scheduled
Posts below are noindex until their launch date; URLs exist for preview only.
- [Fibre Deficiency in Australia: The Cancer Risk Most People Don't Know About](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/fibre-deficiency-australia-bowel-cancer-risk-2026): Almost 20% of bowel cancers in Australia are attributable to insufficient fibre intake. Most Australians eat about half of what they need. What fibre actually does and how to get more of it. _(launches 13 July 2026)_
- [How Much Protein Do Australian Women Actually Need? (Honest Guide 2026)](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/how-much-protein-australian-women-need-honest-guide-2026): TikTok says 130g per day. Dietitians Australia says most women already eat enough. A new CSIRO report says something more nuanced. Here's the actual answer by life stage. _(launches 14 July 2026)_
- [The Gut-Brain Connection: Why Your Digestion Affects Your Mood](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/gut-brain-connection-digestion-mood-mental-health-2026): 90% of the body's serotonin is made in the gut. The link between gut health and mental health is one of the most significant developments in recent nutrition research. What it means in practice. _(launches 15 July 2026)_
- [Why Chronic Stress Makes You Gain Weight (And What to Do About It)](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/stress-weight-gain-cortisol-mechanism-what-to-do-2026): Stress weight gain isn't about eating more. There's a specific hormonal mechanism that stores fat differently when cortisol is chronically elevated. Here's what's actually happening. _(launches 16 July 2026)_
- [Seed Oils: Are They Actually Bad for You? The Honest Australian Answer](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/seed-oils-bad-for-you-honest-research-australia-2026): One side says seed oils are slowly killing us. Mainstream dietitians say they're fine. The honest answer is more nuanced than either camp will admit. Here's what the research actually shows. _(launches 17 July 2026)_
- [Strength Training for Women Over 35: Why the Evidence Has Shifted Away from Cardio](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/strength-training-women-over-35-why-it-matters-2026): For women over 35, strength training protects bone density, maintains metabolic rate, and manages menopausal symptoms more effectively than cardio alone. What changed in the research. _(launches 18 July 2026)_
- [What Happens to Your Body When You Stop Eating Ultra-Processed Food for 30 Days](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/stop-eating-ultra-processed-food-30-days-what-happens-2026): The changes aren't as dramatic as social media makes out, and some are harder than expected. A realistic, research-based look at what actually shifts when you cut out ultra-processed food. _(launches 19 July 2026)_
- [How to Build a Balanced Plate at Every Meal (No Calorie Counting)](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/how-to-build-balanced-plate-no-calorie-counting-2026): Calorie counting works for some people and exhausts most people. A simpler framework for building nutritionally balanced meals using the plate method — practical for everyday Australian eating. _(launches 20 July 2026)_
- [Omega-3 Deficiency in Australia: The Silent Driver of Inflammation](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/omega-3-deficiency-australia-inflammation-food-sources-2026): Omega-3 deficiency is common in Australia as fish consumption declines and processed food rises. What omega-3 actually does, who's most at risk, and the best food sources for Australians. _(launches 21 July 2026)_
- [How to Eat 30 Different Plants Per Week (And Why It Matters for Gut Health)](https://nutrithrive.com.au/blog/30-different-plants-per-week-gut-health-microbiome-2026): Research from the American Gut Project found people eating 30+ different plants per week had significantly more diverse microbiomes. What counts, why diversity matters, and how to get there without overhauling your diet. _(launches 22 July 2026)_

## Business

- Founder: Neer (Melbourne west, Truganina warehouse). Not a TGA-registered health professional.
- ABN: 32 639 442 616
- Email: nutrithrive0@gmail.com
- Address: 15 Europe Street, Truganina VIC 3029, Australia
- All products are food supplements, not medicines. No TGA therapeutic claims are made.
